text: GSM-R networks shall support the GSM telephony teleservice.
GSM-R networks shall support the GSM emergency calls teleservice for 112 calls.
GSM-R networks shall support Voice Group Call Service (VGCS).
GSM-R networks shall support Voice Broadcast Service (VBS).
GSM-R networks shall support Asynchronous 2.4 kbps, 4.8 kbps, and 9.6 kbps transparent bearer services.
GSM-R networks shall support Calling Line Identification Presentation (CLIP).
GSM-R networks shall support Connected Line Identification Presentation (CoLP).
GSM-R networks shall support Call Forwarding Unconditional (CFU).
GSM-R networks shall support Call Forwarding on Mobile Subscriber Busy (CFB).
GSM-R networks shall support Call Waiting (CW).
GSM-R networks shall support Call Hold (HOLD).
GSM-R networks shall support Multi-Party Service (MPTY).
GSM-R networks shall support Closed User Group (CUG).
GSM-R networks shall support Barring of Outgoing International Calls except those to Home PLMN Country (BOIC-exHC).
GSM-R networks shall support Barring of Incoming Calls when Roaming Outside the Home PLMN Country (BIC-Roam).
GSM-R networks shall support Unstructured Supplementary Service Data (USSD).
GSM-R networks shall support Sub-addressing.
GSM-R networks shall support Enhanced Multi-Level Precedence and Pre-emption (eMLPP).
GSM-R networks shall support User-to-User Signalling type 1 (UUS1).
GSM-R networks shall support Functional Addressing.
GSM-R networks shall support Location Dependent Addressing.
GSM-R networks shall support Shunting Mode.
GSM-R networks shall support Multiple Driver Communications.
GSM-R networks shall support Railway Emergency Calls.
The network shall provide a coverage probability of 95% with a coverage level of 38.5 dBuV/m for voice and non-safety critical data.
The network shall provide a coverage probability of 95% with a coverage level of 41.5 dBuV/m on lines with ETCS levels 2/3 for speeds <= 220 km/h.
The EIRENE mobile installation shall operate in networks meeting the specified coverage criteria.
Call setup times defined in EIRENE FRS shall be achieved with authentication and ciphering procedures enabled.
The network shall operate in the R-GSM frequency band (876-915 MHz / 921-960 MHz).
The network shall terminate an ongoing VGCS/VBS call upon receipt of the 3-digit DTMF sequence.
The duration of DTMF tones generated by fixed line dispatchers shall be 70ms +/- 5ms with a minimum gap of 65ms.
Muting and unmuting for VGCS shall comply with EN 301 515 Index [4].
The network shall send a SET-PARAMETER message with attribute "D-ATT = T" to the talking subscriber mobile upon receipt of "###" via DTMF.
The network shall send a SET-PARAMETER message with attribute "D-ATT = F" to the talking subscriber mobile only after receiving "#**" from all talking controllers.
EIRENE mobile stations shall support the GSM-MT air interface conformant to GSM specifications.
All SIM cards used in EIRENE mobiles shall comply with the MORANE FFFIS for GSM-R SIM Cards.
Cab radios shall belong to GSM power class 2 (8W).
General purpose and Operational radios shall belong to GSM power class 4 (2W).
EIRENE radios shall automatically re-attempt Railway emergency call setup for 30 seconds if unsuccessful.
A service availability indication shall be provided to radio users.
The radio shall prevent the user from entering direct mode if GSM service is available.
EIRENE mobile equipment shall operate within a temperature range of -20C to +55C.
EIRENE mobile equipment shall withstand storage temperatures down to -40C.
EIRENE mobile equipment shall operate between altitudes of -100m and 1800m.
All EIRENE mobile equipment shall be protected against shock and vibration in compliance with prEN 50125 and prEN 50155.
The Cab radio shall initiate a call to the controller with 'Railway operation' priority upon MMI action.
The calling driver functional number shall be passed to the network using UUS1.
The Cab radio shall support registration and deregistration of the train number via USSD.
The Cab radio shall provide an audible and visual indication if network registration is unsuccessful.
The Cab radio shall apply call arbitration rules on the basis of call priority.
The SIM card in a Cab radio shall be physically integrated and removable only by maintenance staff.
The Cab radio shall provide an interface for EURORADIO if ERTMS/ETCS communications are required.
The Operational radio shall conform to IP 54 protection standards.
The Operational radio shall withstand semi-sinusoidal shocks of 5g for 100ms.
Every Train Number shall be unique within each GSM-R network for the journey period.
At least one MSISDN number shall be allocated to each mobile station.
Internationally defined short codes starting with CT=1 shall be implemented for mobile originated calls.
The calling party functional number shall be passed to the receiving mobile using UUS1 during call setup.
Railway Emergency call confirmation messages shall be sent with eMLPP priority 4.
Operational radios in shunting mode shall initially select the common shunting group (ID 500).
